blocks.text: DTC P0563: ECU power supply circuit abnormal

When a ground fault occurs between the PGM-FI ECU and PGM-FI main relay 1 (FI MAIN RLY CL-line), the combination meter, ignition coil, and fuel pump power are linked to the ignition operation and turn on and off as usual, but PGM-FI main relay 1 is always The PGM-FI ECU monitors the correlation between the PGM-FI main relay 1 and the ignition operation. If the PGM-FI ECU is still operating after a predetermined period of time in the ignition OFF (LOCK) mode, the PGM-FI ECU determines a fault and stores a DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: 24-hour (e.g. observation, operation (of a machine), care (of patient))
Detection order (of the DTC in question)
2D/C (2 consecutive detection method), PGM-FI warning light: off
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed
12V battery voltage [battery voltage] (PGM-FI ECU's FI MAIN RLY OUT terminal)

blocks.text: If the PGM-FI ECU remains in operation for more than 5 seconds after the ignition is turned to the OFF (LOCK) mode and the self shutdown is completed.

items.text: Ground fault in code (FI MAIN RLY CL-line) between PGM-FI ECU and PGM-FI main relay 1

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to have a fault, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the DTC and freeze data are stored without turning on the PGM-FI warning light.
Temporary DTCs, DTCs and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
